title: Bisect the Broken Build
description: Something broke the test suite between ten commits ago and now. You know the oldest commit was good. Use `git bisect` to identify the exact commit that introduced the regression, then record its SHA in a file called `bad-commit.txt`.
objective: The file `bad-commit.txt` must exist in `/workspace` and contain the full SHA of the commit that first caused `test.sh` to exit non-zero. The SHA must be correct.
hints: Start with `git bisect start`, then mark the current commit bad and the oldest commit good.
Git will check out commits for you to test — run `test.sh` at each step and mark the result.
`git bisect run ./test.sh` automates the entire process if the test is scripted.
After recording the SHA, run `git bisect reset` to return `HEAD` to your branch tip.
